mentions.raw_name: The inn (crossroads inn, near the ruby ford)
Unnamed oarsman on the Titan's Daughter
Unnamed boy at the Saltpans docks
Raff the Sweetling (referenced, on Arya's list)
Ser Ilyn Payne (referenced, on Arya's list)
Ser Meryn Trant (referenced, on Arya's list)
Lord Beric (referenced, in the Tickler's questions echoed by Arya)
The inn (crossroads inn, near the ruby ford)
House Stark (Arya, Sansa, Robb, Jon referenced as Starks)
House Lannister (Tyrion, Cersei, Joffrey's association; Casterly Rock)
House Bracken (fighting around Raventree; "the Brackens are ours now")
The Mountain's men (Polliver, the Tickler, the squire serve Gregor)
The Kingsguard (Sandor references his past white cloak)
Night's Watch (Arya wants to go to the Wall; "The Night's Watch has a castle on the sea")
Sellswords (fled Harrenhal when Gregor's men came)
The Mother / Faith of the Seven (squire cries "Mother have mercy")
Braavosi (the captain and crew of the Titan's Daughter are from the Free City of Braavos)
Iron coin (Jaqen H'ghar's coin, small black iron, worn faceless)
The Hound's longsword (old, nicked, swapped for in the hills)
The Tickler's shortsword, dagger, and throwing knife
Squire's cloak (green, green arrow on white bend)
Sandor's helm (dog-faced, used to boil wine)
Stone cups and pewter platter (at the inn)
Titan's Daughter (Braavosi galley: purple sails, purple hull, gilded prow, two banks of oars, three tall masts)
Dagger robbed off a dying archer (thrown by Arya at the Tickler)
Coppers (thrown on floor by Sandor for payment)

Coins found on the dead: a stag and dozen coppers on Polliver, eight silvers on the squire, couple of pennies on the Tickler, plus a stag in each boot-toe and three golden dragons sewn in Sandor's jerkin lining
"Valar morghulis" (the phrase; Arya uses it as a ritual invocation with the iron coin)
Wolf dream (Arya dreams she is a wolf chasing a riderless horse up a hill with a pack behind her)
Rumors of Sansa using a spell and transforming into a winged wolf (described as popular rumor, not confirmed)
War of the Five Kings (contextual: siege of Riverrun, Joffrey's death, fighting around Raventree, Gregor holding the ford)
Ser (used for Gregor Clegane; the innkeep calls Sandor "ser" and is corrected)
Maester (Maester Luwin referenced; squire asks to be taken to a maester)
"Fear cuts deeper than swords" (Arya's internal mantra, recalled during the fight)
"The gift of mercy" (concept: mercy killing; Sandor asks for it; Arya refuses)
Water dancer's stance (Arya takes the stance instinctively when drawing Needle on Sandor)

Arya's death list (recited: Ser Gregor the Mountain, Dunsen, Raff the Sweetling, Ser Ilyn, Ser Meryn, Queen Cersei — with Polliver, the Tickler, and Joffrey noted as removed; the Hound added then removed ambiguously)
